senses.definition: A person who opposes laws that ban the sale or use of alcoholic drinks.
examples: Many antiprohibitionists argued that banning alcohol would only increase crime.
The party attracted support from antiprohibitionists across the country.

senses.definition: Opposing the legal prohibition of alcoholic drinks.
examples: They launched an antiprohibitionist campaign to change the law.
Antiprohibitionist groups organized rallies in major cities.

senses.definition: A mathematical transformation related to projective geometry that reverses or reflects a projective mapping.
examples: The concept of antiprojectivity appears in advanced studies of projective geometry.
Antiprojectivity can be used to describe certain reflective properties in geometric figures.

senses.definition: A prophet who speaks or acts in opposition to another prophet or to accepted prophecy.
examples: Some followers saw him as a true leader, while others condemned him as an antiprophet.
In the story, the antiprophet challenges the predictions of the established religious leaders.

senses.definition: Designed to act against diseases or conditions of the prostate gland.
examples: Researchers are developing new antiprostate drugs to treat prostate enlargement.
The study focused on antiprostate therapies that reduce inflammation.

senses.definition: Acting against disorders or growth of the prostate gland.
examples: Several antiprostatic agents are used to treat benign prostatic hyperplasia.
The antiprostatic properties of the drug help reduce prostate size.

senses.definition: Opposing prostitution or working to stop it.
examples: The government introduced antiprostitution laws to target sex trafficking.
Antiprostitution campaigns often focus on protecting vulnerable women and children.

senses.definition: A substance that slows or stops the action of proteases, the enzymes that break down proteins.
examples: Antiproteases help protect tissues from damage by controlling enzyme activity.
The drug acts as an antiprotease and is used in some antiviral treatments.

senses.definition: The prevention or slowing down of protein breakdown by enzymes.
examples: Antiproteolysis is important in preventing tissue damage during inflammation.
The compound showed strong antiproteolysis effects in laboratory tests.

senses.definition: The antimatter particle that has the same mass as a proton but the opposite electric charge.
examples: In particle physics experiments, protons can collide and produce an antiproton.
When a proton meets an antiproton, they can annihilate and release energy.

senses.definition: The plural of antiproton, referring to more than one antiproton.
examples: The accelerator stores antiprotons for use in high‑energy collisions.
Scientists observed how antiprotons behave in a magnetic field.

senses.definition: Used to kill or stop protozoa, which are single‑celled organisms that can cause disease.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antiprotozoal drug to treat the infection.
Antiprotozoal agents are important for controlling parasitic diseases.

senses.definition: Opposed to prudence or careful, cautious behavior.
examples: Their antiprudential policies led to serious financial risks.
The article criticizes the government for its antiprudential stance on regulation.

senses.definition: Relieving or preventing itching.
examples: She applied an antipruritic cream to calm the rash.
Antipruritic lotions are often used to treat insect bites.

senses.definition: A drug or cream used to relieve itching.
examples: Your doctor may recommend an antipruritic for the eczema.
This antipruritic works quickly on mosquito bites.

senses.definition: Medicines or products that are used to relieve itching.
examples: Antipruritics are commonly used in the treatment of allergic skin reactions.
Pharmacists can suggest suitable antipruritics for mild rashes.

senses.definition: A person who speaks or writes against psalms or the use of psalms in worship.
examples: He was labeled an antipsalmist for rejecting traditional hymn singing.
The pamphlet, written by an outspoken antipsalmist, criticized the use of psalms in church.

senses.definition: Used to treat skin diseases, especially chronic itching or scaly conditions, in some traditional or alternative medical systems.
examples: In homeopathy, an antipsoric remedy is often chosen for long‑lasting skin problems.
The doctor from the old school prescribed an antipsoric ointment for the patient.

senses.definition: A movement that strongly criticizes traditional psychiatry and its methods.
examples: Antipsychiatry emerged in the 1960s as a reaction to forced treatments.
Some writers in antipsychiatry argue that mental illness is largely a social label.

senses.definition: Relating to a type of medicine used to treat serious mental disorders such as schizophrenia.
examples: The patient responded well to antipsychotic medication.
Antipsychotic drugs can have strong side effects.

senses.definition: A drug used to reduce symptoms of serious mental disorders, such as hallucinations or delusions.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antipsychotic to control her hallucinations.
New antipsychotics tend to have fewer side effects than older ones.

senses.definition: Medicines that are used to treat serious mental health conditions such as schizophrenia and bipolar disorder.
examples: Antipsychotics can help reduce delusions and hallucinations.
Doctors carefully monitor patients who are taking antipsychotics.

senses.definition: A rhetorical figure in which one grammatical case is used instead of another, especially one noun standing in for another related noun.
examples: Antiptosis is sometimes used in classical rhetoric to create emphasis.
The grammarian pointed out an example of antiptosis in the Latin text.

senses.definition: Opposed to modesty, especially about the body or sexual matters.
examples: The author’s antipudic tone shocked more conservative readers.
Some critics described the film as deliberately antipudic.

senses.definition: Strongly opposed to strict moral rules, especially about pleasure and sex, often linked to Puritan ideas.
examples: Her antipuritan views put her in conflict with conservative leaders.
The festival has an openly antipuritan spirit.

senses.definition: Someone who rejects strict Puritan morals and supports a freer way of life.
examples: The writer was known as an antipuritan who mocked narrow religious rules.
As an antipuritan, he defended theater and dancing.

senses.definition: The prevention or slowing down of decay in organic matter.
examples: Salt was used for antiputrefaction long before modern refrigeration.
Antiputrefaction is important in preserving medical samples.

senses.definition: Preventing or slowing down the rotting of organic material.
examples: The solution has antiputrefactive properties and keeps the tissue sample usable.
Antiputrefactive agents are added to some products to extend shelf life.

senses.definition: Acting to prevent or slow down the process of rotting.
examples: The embalming fluid contains antiputrescent chemicals.
Herbs with antiputrescent qualities were used to preserve meat.

senses.definition: Preventing or opposing foul‑smelling decay.
examples: The antiputrid mixture reduced the smell of the waste.
Ancient writings mention antiputrid preparations for treating wounds.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat the formation of pus in infections.
examples: An antipyic treatment was given to control the severe infection.
The doctor applied an antipyic ointment to the wound.

senses.definition: Medicines or treatments that prevent or treat the formation of pus.
examples: In the past, herbal antipyics were used before antibiotics were available.
Surgeons relied on various antipyics to keep wounds clean.

senses.definition: A preparation or drug once used to reduce the formation of pus.
examples: Antipyonin was applied locally to the infected area.
Older medical texts recommend antipyonin for eye infections.

senses.definition: Plural of antipyresis, meaning methods used to reduce fever.
examples: Various antipyreses have been suggested, from cold baths to drugs.
Traditional antipyreses included herbal teas and rest.

senses.definition: The reduction of fever, especially as a medical goal or treatment method.
examples: The main aim of treatment was antipyresis and relief of pain.
Antipyresis can be achieved with both drugs and physical methods.

senses.definition: Reducing fever.
examples: Paracetamol has strong antipyretic and pain‑relieving effects.
The nurse gave the child an antipyretic dose to lower the temperature.

senses.definition: A drug used to reduce fever.
examples: Ibuprofen is a commonly used antipyretic.
The doctor recommended taking an antipyretic every six hours.

senses.definition: Medicines that lower fever.
examples: Antipyretics such as paracetamol are often used for flu symptoms.
Doctors warn against overusing antipyretics in children.

senses.definition: A synthetic drug historically used to reduce pain and fever.
examples: Antipyrin was once a popular remedy for headaches.
Older textbooks mention the use of antipyrin in ear drops.

senses.definition: A drug used especially in the past to relieve pain and reduce fever.
examples: Antipyrine is now rarely used because safer drugs are available.
The formula contains antipyrine and another local anesthetic.

senses.definition: Different preparations or forms of antipyrine‑type drugs.
examples: Chemists developed various antipyrines in the late nineteenth century.
The doctor compared the effectiveness of several antipyrines.

senses.definition: Preventing or relieving burning, especially in the skin.
examples: An antipyrotic cream was applied to the mild burns.
The oil is known for its cooling, antipyrotic effect.

senses.definition: Relating to or derived from antipyrine in chemical compounds.
examples: Antipyryl derivatives are studied for their biological activity.
The chemist synthesized an antipyryl compound for testing.

senses.definition: Written short form for antique or antiquated, used mainly in notes or catalogues.
examples: The label marked the item as antiq, dating from the late nineteenth century.
In the dealer’s list, antiq indicates an older style or make.

senses.definition: A style of typeface with upright, serif letters, often used for body text.
examples: The book was printed in an elegant Antiqua typeface.
Designers often choose Antiqua for long passages of text.

senses.definition: A person who studies, collects, or deals in old and rare objects such as books, coins, or furniture.
examples: An antiquarian sold us a seventeenth‑century map.
The museum works closely with local antiquarians.

senses.definition: Relating to the study, collection, or sale of old and rare things.
examples: He runs an antiquarian bookshop in the old town.
The article appeared in an antiquarian journal.

senses.definition: The interest in or study of old objects, books, and historical details, often for their own sake.
examples: Antiquarianism flourished in Europe in the eighteenth century.
Some critics say his work shows more antiquarianism than deep analysis.

senses.definition: Different forms or examples of antiquarian interest or practice.
examples: The essay compares the antiquarianisms of several nineteenth‑century scholars.
Local antiquarianisms can be seen in the way towns preserve old buildings.

senses.definition: To make something old‑fashioned in style, or to treat it in an overly detailed historical way.
examples: The architect chose not to antiquarianize the design of the new museum.
Some commentators antiquarianize the subject, focusing only on minor details from the past.

senses.definition: In a way that shows detailed interest in old things or historical facts.
examples: He wrote antiquarianly about the village, listing every old house.
The collection is arranged rather antiquarianly, without a clear theme.

senses.definition: Plural of antiquarian, meaning people who study, collect, or deal in old and rare objects.
examples: Antiquarians gathered at the fair to buy and sell rare books.
Local antiquarians helped identify the coins found at the site.

senses.definition: An older word, mainly in British English, for people who study or collect historical objects and details.
examples: In the eighteenth century, many antiquaries recorded local traditions.
The Society of Antiquaries publishes important research on history.

senses.definition: A strong or sometimes excessive interest in old objects or historical details.
examples: The novel has a touch of antiquarism in its long descriptions of old furniture.
Critics accuse the museum of antiquarism instead of telling broader stories.

senses.definition: A place, often in a museum, where ancient objects are kept and displayed.
examples: The city’s antiquarium holds many Roman statues.
Visitors entered the antiquarium to see the oldest artifacts.

senses.definition: The antimatter partner of a quark, with the same mass but opposite charge and other properties.
examples: An antineutron is made of three antiquarks.
When a quark meets an antiquark, they can annihilate each other and release energy.
